UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's public profile within the A.sanp application, storing information not managed by the external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically corresponds to the user's ID from the authentication system.

username.description: A unique, user-chosen handle for identification and mentioning within the app.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for display or contact purposes, not for authentication.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user.

followedUserIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs that this user is currently following. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

followerIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs that are following this user. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents an ephemeral short video post uploaded by a user, which automatically disappears after 48 hours.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this video. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Video)

videoUrl.description: URL of the video content, typically hosted externally (e.g., Cloudinary).

thumbnailUrl.description: URL of a thumbnail image for the video content.

caption.description: Text description or caption provided by the user for the video.

hashtags.description: An array of relevant hashtags associated with the video.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the video was posted, used to calculate its 48-hour expiration.

likeCount.description: The total number of likes this video has received.

commentCount.description: The total number of comments this video has received.

Story.title: Story
Story.description: Represents a short-lived photo or video story uploaded by a user, which disappears after 24 hours.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Story entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this story. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Story)

mediaUrl.description: URL of the media content (photo or video) for the story.

mediaType.description: Specifies whether the story media is an 'image' or 'video'.

caption.description: Optional text overlay or short description for the story.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the story was posted, used to calculate its 24-hour expiration.

Like.title: Like
Like.description: Represents a user's 'like' interaction on a specific video.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Like entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed the like. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Like)

videoId.description: Reference to the Video that was liked. (Relationship: Video 1:N Like)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the like was recorded.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a text comment made by a user on a specific video.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who posted the comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Comment)

videoId.description: Reference to the Video that the comment is on. (Relationship: Video 1:N Comment)

text.description: The actual text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was posted.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) must match the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'followedUserIds' and 'followerIds' for profile display. Owner (request.auth.uid) can write, public can read.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A top-level collection for all public video posts. Each document includes 'userId' for authorization independence. Public read access for the main feed, owner (based on userId field) has write access.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique ID of the video post.

definition.description: Subcollection for user likes on a specific video. Each like document includes 'userId' for authorization independence. Owner (based on userId field) can create/delete their own like, public can read.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent video.

params.name: likeId
params.description: The unique ID of the like interaction.

definition.description: Subcollection for user comments on a specific video. Each comment document includes 'userId' for authorization independence. Owner (based on userId field) can write/delete their own comment, public can read.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent video.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique ID of the comment.

definition.description: Subcollection for ephemeral stories belonging to a specific user. The 'userId' in the path must match request.auth.uid for write operations. Each story document also contains 'userId' for authorization independence. Owner can write, authenticated users can read.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: storyId
params.description: The unique ID of the story post.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for 'A.sanp' is designed to meet the requirements for secure user authentication, a dynamic content feed, and interactive features while adhering to core design principles. The primary goal is to ensure Authorization Independence and support efficient Queries as Authorization Policies (QAPs).

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`)**: User profiles are stored in a top-level collection `users`. Each document's ID (`userId`) directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication `uid` of the user. This path-based ownership makes rules for profile management straightforward: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}` for write operations. Profile data (like username, profile image, follower/followed IDs) is publicly readable to facilitate profile viewing and discovery, which supports QAPs for displaying user profiles.

2.  **Videos (`/videos/{videoId}`)**: Videos are stored in a top-level `videos` collection. This flat structure enables the "Reels Feed" requirement, allowing for efficient global queries (e.g., fetching recent videos for the main feed, ordered by `createdAt`). Authorization Independence is achieved by denormalizing the `userId` of the video uploader directly into each `Video` document. Security rules can then check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` for write operations without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent user document. This also supports QAPs for fetching a user's uploaded videos by querying `videos` collection with `where('userId', '==', <uid>)`.

3.  **Likes (`/videos/{videoId}/likes/{likeId}`)**: Likes are implemented as a subcollection under the specific `Video` they apply to. This maintains a clear logical relationship. Authorization Independence is provided by including `userId` (the liker's ID) in each `Like` document. Rules can verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for creating or deleting a like, ensuring users can only manage their own likes. The `videoId` from the path provides the context, reinforcing that the like is for that specific video. This structure supports QAPs for listing all users who liked a particular video.

4.  **Comments (`/videos/{videoId}/comments/{commentId}`)**: Similar to likes, comments are a subcollection of `Video`. Each `Comment` document includes the `userId` of the commenter for Authorization Independence, allowing rules to enforce that users can only create, update, or delete their own comments (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`). The `videoId` in the path ensures the comment is tied to the correct video. This structure supports QAPs for fetching all comments for a video.

5.  **Stories (`/users/{userId}/stories/{storyId}`)**: Stories are unique to each user and ephemeral, making them a good candidate for a subcollection under the `UserProfile`. This strongly links stories to their owner. The `{userId}` in the path aligns with the `request.auth.uid` for write operations, ensuring only the owner can manage their stories. The `userId` is also present within the `Story` document itself for redundant verification. This supports QAPs for displaying a user's active stories, and the structural segregation ensures a consistent security posture for all stories belonging to a user.

**Authorization Independence**: Every document that requires ownership or membership for write operations (`UserProfile`, `Video`, `Like`, `Comment`, `Story`) explicitly contains the `userId` of the principal responsible for it (or an equivalent map for collaborative data, though not needed here). This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document permissions, making rules simpler, more robust, and less prone to transaction/batching issues.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Policies)**: The collection structures enable efficient queries that inherently respect access patterns. For example, fetching the main video feed is a simple `list` on `/videos` (public read). Fetching a user's own videos or stories involves queries on `userId` fields or path parameters, all allowed by the rule structure. The segregation of public content (`/videos`) from user-specific interactions (`/videos/{videoId}/comments`) and ephemeral content (`/users/{userId}/stories`) ensures that different data types can have distinct, homogeneous security postures, simplifying rule logic and improving query performance for authorized access patterns.
